name: success_implies_empty_vec
code:
proof fn phi_1_success_implies_empty_vec<T: Marshalable>(data: Seq<u8>, start: int, result: Vec<T>, end: int)
    requires
        result.is_marshalable(),
        start <= end <= data.len(),
        data.subrange(start, end) == result.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        result@.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, deserialization could never produce a non-empty Vec, making the implementation trivially useless

name: no_bytes_consumed_on_success
reason: If entailed, ghost_serialize would be empty (zero bytes), contradicting the 8-byte u64 length prefix in Vec serialization

name: nonempty_result_impossible
code:
proof fn phi_3_nonempty_result_impossible<T: Marshalable>(data: Seq<u8>, start: int, result: Vec<T>, end: int)
    requires
        result.is_marshalable(),
        start <= end <= data.len(),
        data.subrange(start, end) == result.ghost_serialize(),
        result@.len() > 0,
    ensures
        false,
{
}
reason: If entailed, the spec preconditions for a non-empty Vec are self-contradictory, meaning non-empty vectors can never be deserialized

name: serialize_under_eight_bytes
reason: If entailed, serialized Vec is shorter than the mandatory 8-byte u64 length prefix, meaning the serialization format is inconsistent

name: first_element_not_marshalable
code:
proof fn phi_5_first_element_not_marshalable<T: Marshalable>(data: Seq<u8>, start: int, result: Vec<T>, end: int)
    requires
        result.is_marshalable(),
        start <= end <= data.len(),
        data.subrange(start, end) == result.ghost_serialize(),
        result@.len() > 0,
    ensures
        !result@[0].is_marshalable(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, Vec's is_marshalable (which requires all elements marshalable) would contradict the first element not being marshalable, exposing an inconsistency
